title: How to add a custom domain in Vercel
summary: A quick walkthrough for pointing your own domain at a Vercel-hosted project.
content:
## Why bother

A custom domain makes your portfolio memorable and signals commitment. Here's how to wire one up in under five minutes.

## Steps

1. Open your project in the Vercel dashboard and go to **Settings → Domains**.
2. Enter your domain (e.g. `yourname.com`) and click **Add**.
3. Vercel shows the DNS records you need. Add them at your registrar:
   - An `A` record pointing `@` to `76.76.21.21`
   - A `CNAME` record pointing `www` to `cname.vercel-dns.com`
4. Wait for propagation (usually a few minutes, up to 48 hours).
5. Vercel auto-issues an SSL cert once DNS resolves.

## Gotchas

- If you use Cloudflare, set the records to **DNS only** (grey cloud) for the initial verification, then you can proxy later.
- Apex domains need the `A` record — `CNAME` on the apex only works on registrars that support `CNAME flattening`.

title: Shipping small is a feature
summary: Why I cut scope aggressively on side projects — and what I learned from shipping a 200-line MVP.
content:
## The default is too big

Every side project I've abandoned had the same cause: I tried to ship v3 before v1.

The fix is to pick the **smallest possible artifact** that answers the question "does anyone care?" — and ship only that.

## What small looks like

- No auth on v1. A link is the auth.
- No database until the JSON file genuinely breaks.
- No settings page until two users ask for the same setting.

## What this buys you

Shipping small gets you to the *feedback loop* fast. That loop — not the code — is the actual product.
